bullets: `secrets-guard.sh` now blocks **tokenised remote URL reads** when the resolved URL embeds `user:secret@host`: `git remote -v`, `git remote show/get-url`, `git config --list/-l/--get remote.*.url`, `cat`/`grep`/`Read` of `.git/config`. Clean repos pass through. Writes (`git remote add`, `git remote set-url`) always pass — they're how the user fixes the leak.

`secrets-guard.sh` now blocks **targeted credential-shaped env-var reads**: `echo $X`, `echo "${X}"`, `printenv X`, and `env|grep <token-keyword>` when `X` matches `*_TOKEN`/`*_SECRET`/`*_API_KEY`/`*_PASSWORD`/`*_PASS` or a well-known literal (`GH_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `AWS_SESSION_TOKEN`). Single-quoted name tokens (`'$GITHUB_TOKEN'`) stay allowed — no interpolation, no leak.

Both extensions apply in all permission modes — credentials leaking into context is a problem regardless of mode. No new settings.json entries; existing `secrets-guard.sh` wiring covers the new surface.
